section_title: The Next Generation: Rising Opera Voices
intro: This list of rising voices was assembled by cross-referencing three lanes of evidence: academic and young-artist programs (Met Lindemann, ROH Jette Parker, San Francisco Adler, Bavarian State Opera Studio), competition results from Operalia 2025 and the 2025 International Opera Awards, and editorial coverage from OperaWire, Bachtrack, Gramophone, and Seen and Heard International. Every singer below has been independently flagged in at least two of those lanes during the 2024-2026 cycle, with verbatim source quotes attached for verification.

rising.name: Mihai Damian
rising.why_rising_quote: This past fall he had a major break through when he took home the top prize at the Operalia competition
rising.recent_milestone: First Prize, Audience Award and Bulgarian Ministry of Culture Prize at Operalia 2025 (Sofia, October 2025)

rising.name: Ekaterine Buachidze
rising.why_rising_quote: paraphrase: Bavarian State Opera Studio member and Hildegard Zadek scholarship holder who shared First Prize at Operalia 2025 and won the Pepita Embil Zarzuela Prize and Rolex Audience Prize.
rising.recent_milestone: Shared First Prize at Operalia 2025 in Sofia (October 2025); member of Bayerische Staatsoper Opernstudio 2024-25

rising.name: Hugh Cutting
rising.why_rising_quote: celebrated for the expressive power of his voice and adventurous musical choices
rising.recent_milestone: Rising Star Award at the 2025 International Opera Awards (Athens); 2025/26 house debuts at ENO (Arsace, Partenope) and Santa Fe Opera (Unulfo, Rodelinda)

rising.name: Adèle Charvet
rising.why_rising_quote: With a voice praised as 'velvety', 'rich' and 'vibrantly sensitive', Charvet has swiftly risen to become one of the most exciting French mezzos
rising.recent_milestone: Rising Star Award at the 2025 International Opera Awards; signed by Intermusica in April 2025; title role debut in Ariodante at Opera national du Rhin

rising.name: Carolina López Moreno
rising.why_rising_quote: In December 2024, when Carolina López Moreno sang the role of Magda in 'La Rondine,' the London Symphony critics proclaimed 'A Star is Born.'
rising.recent_milestone: London Symphony Orchestra debut as Magda in La Rondine under Antonio Pappano (December 2024); Suor Angelica at the BBC Proms 2025

rising.name: Theo Hoffman
rising.why_rising_quote: This past November, American baritone Theo Hoffman became a Grammy nominee for his debut album 'Schubert Beatles'
rising.recent_milestone: Grammy nomination for debut album 'Schubert Beatles' (November 2025); role debut as Oreste in Iphigénie en Tauride at Opéra Comique, Paris (2025)

rising.name: Sandra Hamaoui
rising.why_rising_quote: possessing a fresh voice with a beautiful timbre, rich in colours and nuance
rising.recent_milestone: Wiener Staatsoper debut (September 2025); Breakthrough Artist Award from Palau de les Arts (Valencia)

rising.name: Miles Mykkanen
rising.why_rising_quote: This past fall, he broke out at the Met with his first leading role with the company in 'The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay.'
rising.recent_milestone: First leading Met role as Sammy Klayman in The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay (Fall 2025); Live in HD broadcast scheduled for 2026

rising.name: Edward Nelson
rising.why_rising_quote: In September, he opened the Metropolitan Opera in 'The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay,' becoming one of the standouts of the evening.
rising.recent_milestone: Metropolitan Opera season-opening debut in The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay (September 2025)

rising.name: Alexandra Nowakowski
rising.why_rising_quote: She was a triumph with Gramophone writing, 'From the opening recit...the audience held its breath.'
rising.recent_milestone: Metropolitan Opera debut as Amina in La Sonnambula (2025); graduate of Washington National Opera's Cafritz Young Artist Program

rising.name: Kathleen O'Mara
rising.why_rising_quote: In 2024, Kathleen O'Mara surprised audiences when she took home the first prize and Birgit Nilsson award at the Operalia Competition.
rising.recent_milestone: First Prize at Operalia 2024; First Prize at the 2025 Queen Sonja International Singing Competition

rising.name: Sun-Ly Pierce
rising.why_rising_quote: Pierce was opening the Met's new season in 'The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay,' raising her profile even more.
rising.recent_milestone: First leading Met role as Cherubino in Le Nozze di Figaro (2025); featured in Met season-opener Kavalier & Clay (Fall 2025)

rising.name: Dave Monaco
rising.why_rising_quote: This past fall, Italian tenor Dave Monaco broke out winning third prize as well as the Special Prize by the Bulgarian Ministry of Culture
rising.recent_milestone: Third Prize at Operalia 2025 in Sofia (October 2025), plus Special Prize from the Bulgarian Ministry of Culture

rising.name: Slávka Zámečníková
rising.why_rising_quote: paraphrase: a member of the Vienna State Opera since 2020 who has 'risen rapidly on the world's opera and concert stages and is currently among the most prominent lyric sopranos of her generation.'
rising.recent_milestone: Title role in new production of L'incoronazione di Poppea at Vienna State Opera; Donna Anna and Sophie at the Wiener Staatsoper across 2024-26 seasons

rising.name: Olivia Smith
rising.why_rising_quote: paraphrase: 'Soprano Olivia Smith delivered a vibrant, gloriously sung performance of Mimì in La bohème' at San Francisco Opera's Future Is Now Adler showcase.
rising.recent_milestone: Third-year Adler Fellow at San Francisco Opera (2024-25); Curtis Institute alumna performing Mimì in La bohème

rising.name: Erika Baikoff
rising.why_rising_quote: paraphrase: BBC New Generation Artist and Met Lindemann graduate whose recent highlights include Das Rheingold with Yannick Nézet-Séguin and the Rotterdam Philharmonic.
rising.recent_milestone: BBC New Generation Artist 2024-26; debuts with Bavarian Radio Symphony Orchestra in 2025-26 and Ginevra in Ariodante at Theater an der Wien and Teatro Real
